visualGoal: Make a reader who already owns a racing wheel understand — before they buy anything else — that the wheel is only as good as the sim's analog-axis support, and steer them to the G29 over the G923. Real product proof throughout on the companion stack (rudder, throttle, button box, DIY board, VR, upcoming sim); the two subject wheels stay text-only until verified marine photos exist.

realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: The primary subject of the article, yet text + Amazon-link only. File exists on disk but is NOT in verified-product-images.json, so it must not be embedded. Needs a vision-verified marine-context (or clearly-labeled repurpose) photo added to the manifest before it can become a gearpick/shotgrid image or before certification. PRIMARY certification blocker.

Second subject wheel, text + link only. File on disk but NOT verified. The article's whole point is 'skip this one for marine,' so a photo is lower value than the G29's, but still needed if we ever want a side-by-side visual. Do not embed until verified.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Realistic desk sim-marine bench at dusk with cool blue-teal rim light, a racing wheel clamped to the desk edge shown from behind the seat, an open overlay slot beside it for a real product image, an on-screen ship's helm subtly out of sync with the physical wheel's angle to hint at the center-deadzone problem, no fake logos, no fake UI text, 16:9 editorial cover.
editorialCompositePrompts.negativePrompt: fake brand logos, fake wheelbase, warped controls, impossible ergonomics, tiny unreadable text, procedural gradient art, invented marine hardware

infographicPrompts.title: Analog helm vs. center-deadzone stick
content: Analog-aware sim: small turn = small rudder, wheel tracks your hands
Gamepad-only sim: dead center, non-linear, on-screen wheel out of sync
Fix: x360ce / vJoy present the wheel as a gamepad
Force feedback: unused in every marine sim
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center chart, cool marine palette, large readable text, two-column comparison

infographicPrompts.title: The wheel-as-helm buy order
infographicPrompts.style: Numbered ladder, real product-photo placeholders for Codex overlays, marine colors

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult marine sim curator Gus Calder in a tidy home bridge nook, one hand resting on a desk-clamped racing wheel while the other points at a laptop screen showing a sim's controller-settings page, weathered-sailor-meets-tinkerer energy, product overlay space beside him, practical not fantasy, 4:5 portrait, cool daylight.

screenshotsOrGameMediaNeeded.item: eSail / Sailaway controller-settings screen showing 'enable Controller' + no-FFB note
screenshotsOrGameMediaNeeded.purpose: Illustrate the analog-support check the article tells readers to run; would strengthen the sim-by-sim table.
screenshotsOrGameMediaNeeded.notes: Must be a genuine screenshot if used; do not fabricate a UI. Not required for the current text-first treatment.

openQuestions: Source a vision-verified marine-context photo of the Logitech G29 (and optionally G923) so the article's subject wheels can appear as images rather than text-only — primary path to certification.
Confirm current G29 street price at publish time (sale-dependent ~$180-208 in mid-2026) and update the range if it has moved.
Re-verify Professional Ship Simulator's 9-axis / 50+-button / rudder-deadzone spec against the shipped Early Access build (2026); tighten attribution once the Steam page lists specifics.
If eSail/Sailaway ship a patch adding true 1:1 wheel support or FFB, refresh the sim table and FAQ.
